name: Mabon ap Modron
description: Mabon ap Modron, meaning "Divine Son of the Divine Mother," is a significant figure in Welsh mythology. He is associated with youth, light, and the harvest. His story centers around his abduction as an infant, taken from his mother, Modron (a figure associated with the Mother Goddess Matrona), and imprisoned in an unknown location. Mabon's imprisonment represents the loss of potential and the disruption of the natural order. His rescue is a central quest in the Welsh tale of Culhwch and Olwen, where he is needed to hunt the Twrch Trwyth (a monstrous boar) and retrieve the comb and scissors from its head.  Finding him requires the wisdom and aid of the oldest animals in the world: the Blackbird of Cilgwri, the Stag of Redynfre, the Owl of Cwm Cowlyd, the Eagle of Gwern Abwy, and the Salmon of Llyn Llyw. Each of these creatures directs the questers to the next, eventually leading them to Mabon's prison. Upon his release, Mabon proves to be a skilled hunter and warrior, playing a crucial role in the completion of Culhwch's tasks. He is a symbol of hope, rebirth, and the eventual triumph of good over evil, embodying the cyclical nature of life and the power of knowledge and perseverance. Mabon's story highlights the importance of kinship, the interconnectedness of all living things, and the enduring strength of the ancient Welsh traditions.
subtitle: Divine Son, Lost and Found
achievements: Aided in the hunt for the Twrch Trwyth
Helped retrieve the comb and scissors

keyMyths.title: The Abduction of Mabon
keyMyths.description: Mabon is stolen from his mother, Modron, when he is only three nights old. His disappearance plunges the land into darkness and despair, symbolizing the loss of potential.

keyMyths.title: The Quest for Mabon
keyMyths.description: Culhwch and his companions embark on a perilous quest to find Mabon, seeking the wisdom of the oldest animals. This journey highlights the interconnectedness of the natural world.

keyMyths.title: Mabon's Role in the Twrch Trwyth Hunt
keyMyths.description: Once freed, Mabon is instrumental in hunting the monstrous Twrch Trwyth, showcasing his skill and bravery. He helps retrieve the comb and scissors hidden on the boar.

sources.author: Lady Charlotte Guest
sources.description: A collection of Welsh tales, including Culhwch and Olwen, where Mabon's story is found.
sources.text: A collection of Welsh tales, including Culhwch and Olwen, where Mabon's story is found.
